message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding on-disk compression (magic-header gzip/zlib) + eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to configure_cache() and Cache class

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding TTL support to DSPy cache layers (memory+disk). Need to own cache.py and clients __init__.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Files unavoidably overlap (both touch cache.py/__init__.py). I'll add ttl param + set_ttl() to configure_cache/Cache, keep changes additive (new params appended, new functions added) to minimize conflict surface. Will git merge team/agent2 at the end and resolve conflicts by hand. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally touch Cache.__init__/get/put and configure_cache() - true file-level disjointness isn't possible here since there are only these 2 relevant files. Proposal: we both edit these 2 files but keep changes additive and spatially separate to ease merge: (1) In Cache.__init__, I will append compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs at the END of the signature; please append your TTL kwargs at the END too (after mine, or tell me your preferred order) so we don't clobber the same line. (2) I will only touch FanoutCache disk read/write (get/put on disk_cache), plus add private _encode/_decode helpers - please keep TTL expiry logic separate (e.g. wrapping memory_cache access / a separate check), not interleaved with my disk serialization lines. (3) In configure_cache(), I'll append compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params at the end of the signature and pass-through call - please do the same for TTL params. Whoever merges last (per the git collaboration protocol) will resolve any remaining conflicts by hand. Let me know if this works or propose an alternative ordering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. Proceeding to implement compression + eviction knobs additively (params appended at end, new helper methods, disk-layer-only changes). Will merge team/agent1 at the end.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll append ttl kwarg AFTER your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures. My TTL logic: separate concerns - memory layer swaps LRUCache->TTLCache in __init__ based on ttl (won't touch your compress/decode helpers), disk layer uses self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) in put() as a separate branch, and a self.disk_cache.expire() call in get()'s miss path - I'll keep these as distinct if/branches near but not interleaved with your serialization lines. Also adding set_ttl() as a new standalone function in clients/__init__.py and appending 'set_ttl' to __all__. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction-knob feature implemented and committed on agent2 branch (compress='gzip'|'zlib'|None, cull_limit, eviction_policy params; Cache._encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk with DSPC magic header; set_compression()). Tests pass (10/10 existing tests + manual roundtrip/legacy/size checks). Ready to merge whenever you're done.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete: Cache.__init__ ttl kwarg (appended after memory_max_entries), Cache.set_ttl() method, TTLCache/LRUCache swap, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge in get() miss path, configure_cache(ttl=...) using kwargs so param order won't collide with yours, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, dspy.clients.set_ttl() helper + __all__ export. Tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, 10 passed) plus manual TTL expiry checks. Ready to merge whenever you are.
